title: Adjusting: Prepaid Insurance
event: On October 1, Reyes Co. paid $12,000 for a 12-month insurance policy and debited Prepaid Insurance. Prepare the December 31 adjusting entry.

lines.label: 12,000 × 3/12
lines.why: Oct–Dec = 3 months of coverage consumed at $1,000/month.
lines.trap: Count the months on your fingers. Oct 1 to Dec 31 is 3 months — not 2, not 12.

lines.label: asset used up
lines.why: The asset shrinks as coverage is used; $9,000 remains for next year.
lines.trap: Credit the ASSET, not Cash — cash moved back in October. Adjusting entries never touch Cash.

memorize.body: Expense = cost × months used ÷ total months. The prepaid balance = what's LEFT.
Adjusting entries never touch Cash. The adjustment only fixes the TIMING of the expense.

questions.prompt: $12,000 policy bought Oct 1 covers 12 months. Insurance expense at Dec 31 = ?

distractors.feedback: You expensed the whole payment. Only the 3 months USED (Oct–Dec) belong to this year.
That's what REMAINS prepaid. The adjusting entry records what was USED: 3 months = 3,000.
That's one month. Three have passed since October 1.

build.scaffold: Months used ÷ months bought × cost. Debit the expense for what's used; credit the asset down.
